data.title: As a practitioner, I can trust that follow-on epics receive the same scrutiny as the primary epic
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update brainstorm's Follow-on Epic Gate to run the full scrutiny pipeline on each follow-on epic before ticket creation, with a depth cap to prevent recursive loops.
**Why**: Follow-on/split epics currently bypass the scrutiny pipeline — they get a lightweight approval gate but no fidelity review, feasibility check, or scenario analysis, allowing underspecified epics to enter preplanning.
**Scope**:
- IN: Run full scrutiny pipeline on each follow-on epic in Phase 3 Step 0. Add follow_on_depth parameter (max 1) to prevent recursive scrutiny. Add request_origin parameter (user vs scope-split) to suppress Part A artifact gap analysis for scope-split epics. Follow-on epics of follow-on epics become ticket stubs for user approval without re-entering scrutiny.
- OUT: Changes to the scrutiny pipeline itself (shared with Stories 1 and 3). Planning-intelligence log (Story 4).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, each follow-on epic created during brainstorm Phase 3 Step 0 passes through the full scrutiny pipeline (gap analysis, web research, scenario analysis, fidelity review) before its ticket is created
  ← Satisfies: "follow-on or split epics pass through the full scrutiny pipeline before ticket creation"
- When this story is complete, follow-on depth is capped at 1 — follow-on epics of follow-on epics are presented as ticket stubs for user approval without re-entering scrutiny
  ← Satisfies: "follow-on depth parameter (max 1) prevents recursive loops"
- When this story is complete, scope-split follow-on epics (request_origin: scope-split) skip Part A artifact gap analysis in the scrutiny pipeline
  ← Satisfies: "follow-on gap analysis uses wrong reference mitigated by request_origin parameter"
- When this story is complete, the follow_on_depth value is exposed as a named state variable for the planning-intelligence log to consume
  ← Satisfies: "planning-intelligence log records follow-on scrutiny engagement"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow-on epics are processed one-at-a-time (not batched) — the full scrutiny pipeline runs serially for each follow-on. This is the existing interaction model from the Follow-on Epic Gate.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
